title: lecananthus peduncularis
intro:
Lecananthus peduncularis, also known as 'Lecanthus', is a plant species within the family Apocynaceae, however I can't find any more information about it. 

However, I can suggest a similar species called 'Lecanthus' which is also known as 'Lecanthus peduncularis' but is a synonym of the genus 'Carissa' (Carissa carandas), also known as 'Carissa' or 'Cerissa' which is a genus of flowering plants in the family Apocynaceae. 

Carissa carandas is a shrub or small tree native to tropical and subtropical regions, and it is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. 

Carissa carandas has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and indigestion, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities.
